notes: Twenty-eighth validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its mission says the school aims to create a 21st Century learning environment that recognizes multiple intelligences and diverse learning styles.
Official materials say the school uses a culturally responsive curriculum with diverse instructional strategies and enriching content.
Its vision says every child is a genius in at least one domain of intelligence.
Recent school news highlights a third-place finish in the Lee County Science Olympiad.
Administrative pages list Olivia Lyon as Head of School.
Board materials identify John D. Werderman as chairman of the board.
Its homepage rotates lines such as Inspiring Passion, Igniting Success and Developing Leaders, Fostering Community.
Official history says the school was founded in 1993 as Chatham Academy before receiving a charter in 1996.
The school says it was among the first group of North Carolina schools to receive a charter in 1996.
Official materials say the school has been nationally accredited since 2006 and most recently went through reaccreditation in 2021 through Cognia.
Its published mission says the school establishes and maintains a challenging learning environment that respects individual learners and prepares them to succeed in a globally competitive society.
Board materials list Erik Christenbury as president.
Its public materials emphasize the phrase High Expectations in Academics, Behavior and Character.
The school's mission says it aims to positively provide a safe and quality education with high expectations so students can excel and grow.
Official history says founder Hilda Hicks opened the school in 1998 and led it for 28 years.
The school publishes an axiom that students need great teachers and principals each year, with high expectations matched by rigorous academic instruction.
Official leadership pages identify Danielle Hicks Baptiste as Executive Director.
Board materials list Pastor Janet Ward as chair.
Official history says the school was formerly known as M.I. Day.
Its mission says students should strive not just for strong marks, but to become better people and make their world a better place.
The school says character education is integrated throughout the curriculum, extracurricular activities, and daily social interactions.
Official college-readiness materials note a partnership with Belmont Abbey through something called The Voyager Program.
Current school profile materials list Kristen Lanier as both Principal of Upper School and Principal of Lower School.
Board materials list Steve Jones as chair.
Its homepage uses the phrase Learning for Liberty.
The school says classical education is meant to form intelligent, virtuous citizens.
Official materials identify Scholarship, Character, and Community as the school's three pillars.
Its vision emphasizes an honest search for universal truth, goodness, and beauty.
The school says good citizenship includes logical argument, respect for the rule of law, and appreciation of American constitutional democracy.
Official staff pages list Paul Rezzo as headmaster.
